user_outcome: Running `sworn models` lists, per linked provider, the models actually available on the user's account — grouped by the prefix the user would type — with capability annotations sourced only from wire-reported metadata (tools: yes/no/unknown), so explicit-prefix resolution ships with its discoverability counterpart and unknown never counts as capable.
rationale: Delivers N-11 (decided in-release 2026-07-02): the explicit-prefix bargain trades away magic, so discovery must be one command. Capability metadata over the wire is heterogeneous (verified during planning): OpenRouter /api/v1/models reports supported_parameters (including 'tools'), modality, pricing, context; Mistral /v1/models reports a capabilities object (function_calling, vision); Ollama /api/show reports a capabilities list on recent versions; Google models.list reports supportedGenerationMethods (partial — tool support not explicit); OpenAI, Groq, and Anthropic return bare ID lists. The catalog therefore annotates honestly and fail-closed: only wire-reported facts, 'unknown' everywhere else, and NO paid probe calls — listing uses models/list endpoints only. Availability of a provider comes from registry enumeration (S05). Late-deferrable as its own slice per the planning decision.

effort_complexity.rationale: Known-shape HTTP listing per provider + normalisation + a CLI command; breadth across ~6 provider endpoints with canned-fixture tests.

risks.description: Provider models-endpoint shapes drift or differ by account tier.
risks.mitigation: Parse defensively with canned-fixture tests per provider; unknown fields ignored; a shape mismatch degrades that provider's annotations to unknown rather than failing the whole command.

risks.description: Temptation to infer capability from model-name heuristics (e.g. assuming every gpt-* supports tools).
risks.mitigation: AC-02 forbids it: annotation source is wire metadata only; heuristics are a spec violation, not a nice-to-have.

acceptance_criteria.text: When `sworn models` runs, it SHALL query the models/list endpoint of each provider whose credentials are configured (determined via registry enumeration/availability, no dispatch) and print the available model IDs grouped by the resolution prefix the user would type (e.g. openrouter/, mistral/, ollama/), with a --provider flag filtering to one provider (TestModelsCommand with canned fixtures).

Capability annotations SHALL be sourced exclusively from wire-reported metadata (OpenRouter supported_parameters, Mistral capabilities, Ollama /api/show capabilities, Google supportedGenerationMethods); providers returning bare ID lists (OpenAI, Groq, Anthropic) SHALL be annotated tools=unknown; no name-pattern heuristics SHALL be used (table-driven TestCatalogAnnotations covers one fixture per provider class).

If a provider's models endpoint fails (auth, network, non-2xx), `sworn models` SHALL print a per-provider error naming the provider and cause, continue listing the remaining providers, and exit non-zero only when EVERY configured provider failed.

The command SHALL make no completion/dispatch/probe calls — models/list endpoints only (asserted in tests by a transport recorder that fails on any non-list path); `go build ./...` and `go test ./cmd/sworn/... ./internal/model/...` SHALL pass.
